title: Uutensil Stirr - the Unique Automatic Pan Stirrer - Longer Nylon Legs, Olive Green
description:
About this item
Automatic stirrer with unique and innovative design - Saves time when you are cooking - There's no need to spend time manually stirring your soups, sauces, porridge, gravy, and more, the stirrer will do the job for you
Hands-free self-rotating with three speed options - Simply place it in a pan or pot, push the button to turn it on, and the stirrer will automatically rotate by itself - Push the button again to turn if off - Moves forward and backward during operation
Features removable nylon legs - Heat-resistant up to 120°C, safe for non-stick pans and pots, food and dishwasher safe
Cordless and battery operated - Requires 4 pieces of AA batteries - Batteries are not included
Stirrer dimensions: 8.8 x 4.6 x 4.6 inches - Color: olive green (body) x grey (legs)
reviews_5s:
The Uutensil Stirr works great and is easy to use!
I set it on the second speed and it stirs my yogurt milk great during the 1 hour heating process.
And cleaning is a breeze since the bottom part that is doing the stirring just pops off for easy cleaning.
This is a LOT better than me whisking the milk for an hour.
I would imagine that for a thicker soup, I might have to use the third setting.
Enjoy!!!This gadget is Awesome!! Easy to use and easy to clean. Saves a lot of time off your feet when you have recipes that need constant stirring.

Every cook should have one.

You won’t regret the purchase.Un muy buen producto de buena calidad, funciona de maravillas. Es de baterias y se separa para poder lavarlo facilmente.Play Video
It will Stir your lighter sauces.
It is definitely not for mashed potatoes or even a Mac and cheese.

It will vibrate your pot off of the burner. So you have to watch it, but at least you don't have to sit there and Stir your gravys for a long time. Gravy's is mainly what I use it for. I can recommend it.She loves it. She cooks all the time and said she gave it a few manual stirs the first couple of times but then she stopped because the product works great. She’s hard to shop for so I’m really pleased that she liked it.This is perfect for stirring your gravy or sauces while you use your hands for something else.
A friend was over, saw and loved it. I purchased this one for her. She's used it a lot and recommended it to friends. Buy two...give one away!This is for a gift for daughters birthday in July. I have a different brand which she thought was such a great idea. I tried to buy the one I have and it was unavailable but this one looks even sturdier and I love mine. I know she'll love it.It works so far out of the box, and I can't wait to try it in my cooking. They sent me the wrong color, but I have a feeling I'm going to be very happy with this purchase. If I run into any issues, I'll be sure to come back and update my review.It stirs easily and I like it, I use it alot BUT I can't remove the battery top so I cannot take the stirrer part off to wash thoroughly.Best everIm right handed.And its disabilited so this is a life line for me when it comes too cooking. Love it couldn't live with out one
reviews_1s:
This stir doesn’t stir. It just makes a lot of noise and sits there and vibrates in the same spot. Great idea, terrible product.This is a piece of junk. Hasn't worked right once. Shuts off and now won't work at all!! Do not waste your money!!! Wanted to leave zero stars but wouldn't let me.Fell over, popping open the battery compartment and dropping the batteries into my food!It vibrates but does not stir the entire bottom of the pot.So I've been ridiculed for buying this item as I barely even cook, but thought hey when i make soup I can use it so my soup doesn't burn.

Well I have tried to stop the ridicule by trying to get this thing to work several times and it is my conclusion that the only thing this can stir is broth. I've used it in ramen, the noodles stopped it from spinning, I've used it in corn soup, the corn cob was in and cut up into inch cuts, this thing could not turn them or push them out of the way. (The pot was about 4 times the size of the Stirr)

The only time i've gotten it to spin is when i put it in boiling water (or anything without any additives, no leafy greens nothing) So if you're making broth without any solid ingredients it will work, otherwise buy a spoon, it'll stress you out less.The packaging it came in was smashed - no big deal, not their issue. But if your cooktop is even slightly sloped and you don’t know it, there’s no hope of this moving around much, much less actually stirring the bottom of the pot. It also proceeded to melt since it didn’t move around fast enough, and it fell in the food multiple times and splashed hot liquid everywhere. The only redeeming quality was that it gave everyone a good laugh as I sent them a video of it in action (before it melted but after it fell in) to show them what present I was going to get for my mom.Legs melted after a couple uses.The damn thing would not turn on. I even checked the expiration date on the batteries, no problem with that.
I decided to go to YouTube and see if I could find any helpful videos so as to see if I was doing something wrong. There's a hilarious video of some woman on a news network, trying to make her Uutensil Saucepan Stirrer work too. It would budge... Guess I'm not the only one.I was hoping this would be a cool little gadget to use but it melted the first time I used it.The legs melted when I put it in a pot of soup wouldn’t turn
